name: -p
options.description: Port to connect to on the remote host

name: -i
options.description: Identity file (private key) for public key authentication

name: -l
options.description: Login name on the remote machine

name: -o
options.description: Set an option in ssh_config format (e.g. StrictHostKeyChecking=no)

name: -L
options.description: Local port forwarding: [bind_address:]port:host:hostport

name: -R
options.description: Remote port forwarding: [bind_address:]port:host:hostport

name: -D
options.description: Dynamic (SOCKS) port forwarding on the given local port

name: -W
options.description: Forward stdin/stdout to host:port over the secure channel (tunnel mode)

name: -J
options.description: Connect via a jump host (ProxyJump): [user@]host[:port]

name: -F
options.description: Use an alternative per-user configuration file

name: -b
options.description: Bind to this address on the local machine for the connection

name: -c
options.description: Select the cipher specification for encrypting the session

name: -m
options.description: Specify MAC (message authentication code) algorithms

name: -N
options.description: Do not execute a remote command (useful for port forwarding only)

name: -f
options.description: Go to background just before command execution

name: -v
options.description: Verbose mode — print debugging messages

name: -vv
options.description: More verbose — increased debug output (level 2)

name: -vvv
options.description: Maximum verbosity — full debug output (level 3)

name: -X
options.description: Enable X11 forwarding

name: -Y
options.description: Enable trusted X11 forwarding (bypasses X11 security extension controls)

name: -C
options.description: Request compression of all data

name: -A
options.description: Enable forwarding of the authentication agent connection

name: -t
options.description: Force pseudo-terminal allocation

name: -T
options.description: Disable pseudo-terminal allocation

name: -q
options.description: Quiet mode — suppress most warning and diagnostic messages
Force IPv4 addresses only
Force IPv6 addresses only

name: -G
options.description: Print the configuration that would be used for the given host and exit

name: -n
options.description: Redirect stdin from /dev/null (prevents reading from stdin)

name: -a
options.description: Disable forwarding of the authentication agent connection

name: -x
options.description: Disable X11 forwarding

name: -K
options.description: Enable GSSAPI-based authentication and forwarding

name: -k
options.description: Disable forwarding of GSSAPI credentials

name: -M
options.description: Place the connection into master mode for connection sharing

name: -S
options.description: Specify the location of a control socket for connection sharing

name: -e
options.description: Set the escape character (default: ~); use 'none' to disable

name: -E
options.description: Append debug logs to a file instead of stderr

args.name: destination
args.description: Remote host to connect to, in the form [user@]hostname

args.name: command
args.description: Command to execute on the remote host (optional)
